itemListElement.name: Ford Duratorq ZSD-422 / DT224 PUMA / TDC4

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender 2.2 TDCi engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 2.2 TDCi engine typically costs £2,600–£4,800 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£1,500 depending on your location and workshop. A brand new stripped engine is available at approximately £4,765. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Defender.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender 2.2 TDCi engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 2.2 TDCi is the most powerful engine ever fitted to the classic Defender and is capable of high mileages when properly maintained. However, it introduced modern diesel complexities — EGR coolers, variable-geometry turbos, and chain-driven timing. Reliability depends entirely on maintenance history. Well-maintained examples are robust; neglected ones suffer EGR, turbo, injector, and timing chain issues.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Defender 2.2 TDCi?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are EGR cooler leaks (subject to TSB LR-TIS-2014-077), turbo actuator failure, injector seal leaks, and timing chain tensioner wear. The engine is chain-driven, not belt-driven.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender 2.2 TDCi engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £18,000 Defender, a £3,500 recon is 19% of the car's value — definitely worth it. Against a tired £6,000 example, the same recon approaches our 60% Land Rover threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender 2.2 TDCi engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 2.2 TDCi Puma engine carries several codes: Ford ZSD-422 (Ford designation), DT224 PUMA (Land Rover parts code), or TDC4 (engine family code). All refer to the same 2.2-litre Ford Duratorq engine used in the 2012–2016 Defender.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — and this is the good news. The 2.2 TDCi is a non-interference engine. Even if the timing chain snaps, the pistons and valves will not come into contact. A snapped chain doesn't mean the engine is destroyed — but it does require a full strip-down to replace the chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately to avoid the labour bill of a full rebuild.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Defender 2.2 TDCi Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the 2.2 TDCi Puma engine. A brand new stripped 2.2 TDCi engine is available at approximately £4,765 supply only.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender 2.2 TDCi engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare 2.2 TDCi engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: EGR Cooler Leaks
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, occasional burning smell, engine management light.
problems.recommendation: This is one of the most frequently reported issues on the 2.2 TDCi Puma, and was subject to a Land Rover TSB (LR-TIS-2014-077) for an EGR cooler upgrade. If you're experiencing unexplained coolant loss, have the EGR cooler inspected promptly — ignoring it can lead to overheating and eventual engine damage, though it doesn't typically progress to full engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

problems.title: Injector Seal Leaks
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, diesel smell in the cabin, increased fuel consumption, black smoke from exhaust.
problems.recommendation: The 2.2 TDCi uses VDO/Continental injectors and injector seal failures are a well-documented pattern. If caught early, seal replacement is straightforward. Ignored, leaking injector seals can cause carbon buildup and lead to more expensive injector or cylinder head repairs. Regular inspection during servicing is recommended.

failureLink.label: Read the full injector seal failure guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the engine, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,500 (chain + tensioner replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The 2.2 TDCi is chain-driven, not belt-driven. While there's no official replacement interval — chains are often "deemed good for the life of the engine" — tensioner wear is a known pattern. The silver lining: this is a non-interference engine. Even if the chain snaps, the pistons and valves will not come into contact. However, a snapped chain still leaves you stranded and requires a full strip-down to replace guides, sprockets, and tensioner — a labour-intensive job. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain and tensioner inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender 2.2 TDCi — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before it causes overheating damage
Straightforward fix if caught before carbon buildup damages the head
Preventative — far cheaper than a full strip-down if the chain snaps
Specialist repair available; cheaper than a full turbo replacement
Non-interference design means valves survive — but labour-intensive strip-down required

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 2.2 TDCi? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented EGR cooler replacement or TSB completion (LR-TIS-2014-077)
* Full service history with 12,000-mile (or less) oil changes using Ford WSS-M2C913-C spec oil
* No cold-start rattle from the timing chain tensioner
* Injector service history — seals replaced or inspected within the last 40,000 miles
avoidIf: * No proof of EGR cooler maintenance or recall completion
* Cold-start rattle with no documented timing chain inspection
* Poor service history — this engine is unforgiving of neglected oil changes
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — EGR, turbo, or injector faults can be expensive to trace

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 2.2 TDCi Puma is the most powerful and refined engine ever fitted to the classic Defender, and when properly maintained it's capable of high mileages. But it's a modern diesel with modern diesel problems — EGR coolers, turbos, injectors, and timing chains all need attention. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained 2.2 is a £25,000 classic; a neglected one is a £5,000 project.

cta.label: → Compare 2.2 TDCi engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender 2.2 TDCi (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or metallic ticking on startup (timing chain tensioner wear warning)
* Service History — including the 2.2 TDCi era (2012–2016) and confirmed oil-change discipline using Ford WSS-M2C913-C spec oil
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR cooler coolant leak
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, hesitation, or whistling? (turbo actuator failure warning)
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate DPF clogging on this engine
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved EGR, turbo, or injector issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (DPF, EGR, or injector-related)
